assumptions: The developer uses macOS, Linux, or Windows.
The general Quickstart requires project source that Fly Launch can scan or a Dockerfile; the HelloFly candidate instead uses Fly.io's pre-built image.
The developer can open a browser from the CLI authentication flow; Microsoft WSL may require Fly.io's documented xdg-open symbolic link.

developer_goal: Get up and running on Fly.io by launching a first app.

first_success.outcome: HelloFly demo app is deployed; fly status succeeds and fly apps open loads the fly.dev URL.
first_success.observable_signal: After `fly status` and `fly apps open`, the HelloFly app is reachable at `https://<my-app-name>.fly.dev/`, and the guide states that the first app was successfully launched.

route_selection.surface: Launch the official HelloFly demo app
route_selection.rule: Prefer the official first-deploy tutorial path without duplicating steps or splitting form fields into separate required path steps.
route_selection.selected: Launch the official HelloFly demo app

candidates.name: Launch the official HelloFly demo app
candidates.reason: Fly.io Launch a demo app (HelloFly) is the selected first success. Removed the parallel own-app launch path that previously duplicated the journey and inflated counts.

required_path.action: Open the official Fly.io Getting started page.
required_path.observable_result: Launch hellofly demo app is visible.
The guide's numbered flow is visible.

required_path.action: Install flyctl with the documented command for the local operating system.
For a new account, run `fly auth signup`.
Create the account using email, Google, or GitHub.
required_path.observable_result: The flow returns to the command line ready to use Fly.io.

required_path.action: If prompted before deployment, provide a valid credit card.
required_path.observable_result: The account can continue launching resources.
`Do you want to tweak these settings before proceeding? (y/N)` appears.

required_path.action: Choose whether to accept or customize the proposed launch settings.
required_path.observable_result: The launch proceeds with confirmed settings.

required_path.action: If customization was selected, change settings and click `Confirm Settings`.
required_path.observable_result: The web flow confirms settings and returns control to launch.

required_path.action: Create the app, write and validate `fly.toml`, retrieve the image, provision IPs, and launch the app Machines.
required_path.observable_result: The output ends with `Visit your newly deployed app at https://<my-app-name>.fly.dev/`.
The new app and its Machine status are listed.
HelloFly is reachable, and the guide explicitly declares first-app success.

prerequisites.description: A local macOS, Linux, or Windows environment that can run the documented operating-system-specific flyctl installation command.
Browser access for the signup or login flow started by flyctl.
A Fly.io account, created during the path or an existing account used to sign in.

The demo guide says a new account starts on Pay As You Go and that a credit-card prompt occurs at some point. Current trial documentation permits limited access without a payment method until 2 total VM hours or 7 days, whichever comes first, subject to resource limits.
flyctl installed and available as the `fly` command.
For the general Quickstart route, an existing app source directory or Dockerfile that Fly Launch can inspect.

external_gates.description: flyctl is mandatory, with operating-system-specific installation and possible PATH setup.
The CLI launches a browser-based signup or login flow; WSL may require a symbolic-link fix for browser launch.
GitHub signup adds an email confirmation and password-setting step for later active verification.
The demo guide says a credit-card prompt occurs at some point, while the current free trial may defer it. Trial exhaustion stops apps and blocks new launches or deploys until billing is configured.
Fly Launch proposes project-dependent settings, and the HelloFly candidate explicitly requires accepting defaults or entering web customization.
When `fly launch` deploys the app, that deployment must complete before status and browser verification.
For non-obvious deployment failures, Fly.io documents `fly version update`, `fly doctor`, `fly config validate`, `fly logs`, and optional debug or SSH diagnostics.

platform_outcomes.description: HelloFly demo app is deployed; fly status succeeds and fly apps open loads the fly.dev URL.

excluded.reason: Path compacted to the official first-success tutorial structure.

sources.title: Getting started
evidence_supported: Quickstart, demo, framework, and deeper alternatives

sources.title: fly auth signup
evidence_supported: Signup command opens a browser form

sources.title: Troubleshoot your deployment
evidence_supported: Deployment failure recovery commands and common failure classes

sources.title: Create an app with Fly Launch
evidence_supported: Fly Launch scans source, proposes settings, may hand control to a framework scanner, and may require a later deploy

sources.title: Autostop/autostart Machines
evidence_supported: Fly Launch apps default to automatic Machine stop and start behavior
Incoming requests can start existing stopped Machines

uncertainties.question: At exactly which point does a new account receive the credit-card prompt?
uncertainties.impact: The number and order of billing actions before first deployment can vary by account eligibility and trial state.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: The demo guide says only `at some point`, while the trial page explains when payment becomes necessary without locating the prompt in the launch flow.

uncertainties.question: Does the general own-app Quickstart always deploy during `fly launch`?
uncertainties.impact: Some projects add a conditional `fly deploy` action before first success.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: The Quickstart says `fly launch` deploys most apps and instructs the developer to run `fly deploy` if prompted; the exact branch depends on project detection.
